case_title: JAMES ANGELL VS. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. BASED ON STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF'S ORAL MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS GRANTED. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BY MARCH 21, 2007; PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY WHICH CONTRACTS ARE WRITTEN, WHICH ARE ORAL AND THE TERMS OF EACH. COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BY MARCH 21, 2007. COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Ninth Cause Of Action For Negligent Misrepresentation Contained In Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DEMIAN I. OKSENENDLER, ESQUIRE (MANNION & LOWE, 415.733.1054) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MATTHEW K. GOOD, ESQUIRE (CODDINGTON HICKS & DANFORTH, 650.592.5400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CONTAINED IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. BASED ON STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF'S ORAL MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS GRANTED. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Mar-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER.
APPEARANCES: DEMIAN I. OKSENENDLER, ESQUIRE (MANNION & LOWE, 415.733.1054) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MATTHEW K. GOOD, ESQUIRE (CODDINGTON HICKS & DANFORTH, 650.592.5400) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BY MARCH 21, 2007; PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY WHICH CONTRACTS ARE WRITTEN, WHICH ARE ORAL AND THE TERMS OF EACH. COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BY MARCH 21, 2007. COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
